name: Baked Scallops w/ Mushrooms & Pablano Peppers
recipeIngredient: A large bunch of Fresh Basil, minced
A small bunch of Fresh Rosemary, minced
A bunch of Fresh Thyme, minced
Several dashes White Worcestershire Sauce, to taste
Sea scallops, halved or whole (1/2 lb)
recipeInstructions:
For Sauce:
1. Pour cream into a medium saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add White Worcestershire Sauce, white pepper, and minced herbs.
3. Reduce the mixture until the desired consistency is reached.
4. Adjust seasonings to taste while the sauce simmers.
For Scallops:
1. Cut the tops off the peppers, remove seeds and membranes.
2. Blanch the peppers for a minute or two in boiling water, then cool in cold water.
3. In a heavy skillet, melt 1 tablespoon butter over high heat.
4. Add mushrooms and minced garlic; sauté until mushrooms are almost done.
5. Remove to a bowl, reserving scallops on paper towels to pat dry.
6. In the same skillet, add the remaining butter, increase heat to high.
7. When butter is melted but not browned, add scallops in batches, sautéing and stirring often until coated, about 3-4 minutes per side.
8. Return mushrooms and garlic to the skillet; mix well.
9. Stuff peppers with the scallop/mushroom mixture.
10. Arrange peppers in a baking dish (a glass brownie pan works well).
11. If there is leftover stuffing, fill gaps between peppers.
12. Cover with sauce, sprinkle with paprika, and bake at 350°F for 30-45 minutes.
For Serving:
1. Serve scallops over spinach angel hair or other delicate pasta.
2. Top each serving with extra stuffing and sauce.
3. Offer freshly grated Parmesan cheese on the side.
4. Complement with garlic bread, a green salad, and a well-chilled white or rosé wine.
